\noindent In this Chapter we will work with the \ttblue{inventoryData} data file, that serves as the sample frame for the \emph{Case: Valuation of inventories}. The data file is included with the \ttblue{FSaudit} package. The data file indicates for each of the 3,500 elements in the inventories population its \ttblue{id}, a sequential number from 1 to 3,500, the book value $x$ is \ttblue{bv}, and we have added an audit value $y =$ \ttblue{av} to enable running simulations on the file to compare different sampling strategies.

\noindent Details of the data file can be obtained using base \ttblue{R} functionality.

\par\addvspace{\topsep}
\begingroup
\fvset{listparameters={%
  \setlength{\topsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\partopsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\parsep}{0pt}%
  \setlength{\itemsep}{0pt}%
}}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
# Open FSaudit library
library(FSaudit)

# Show the variable names of the data file
names(inventoryData)
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
[1] "item"   "bv"     "av"     "av_mus" "av_reg" "av_dif"
[7] "av_rat"
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
# Get the number of rows
(N <- nrow(inventoryData))
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
[1] 3500
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
# Calculate the total book value
sum(inventoryData$bv)
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
[1] 7360816
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_blue}]
# Show the top-6 rows with the first three columns
head(inventoryData[1:3])
\end{Verbatim}
\begin{Verbatim}[breaklines=true,formatcom=\color{ada_light_blue}]
  item      bv      av
1    1  120.60  109.92
2    2 2600.10 2219.24
3    3 4899.90 6398.96

\end{Verbatim}
\endgroup
